item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Nicely done and an intriguing read from the very beginning.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: General note, if you'd like to let an author know that you're referencing their work in one of your pieces, sending them a direct wikidot PM is more reliable than using a forum comment.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I dont know if you check these, but I wrote a cameo for your SCP in mine and will be posting it soon. If you have any issues with my portrayal let me know, it’s my first skip!

http://scp-sandbox-3.wikidot.com/zenobiyl

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Due to licensing issues, the image for SCP-4586 has been removed and is now replaced by a new one.

Source:

+ show block
– hide block

https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:(1845-1880)_%CE%92%CF%81%CE%B1%CE%BD%CE%B1%CE%BA%CE%B7%CF%82_%CE%93%CE%B5%CF%89%CF%81%CE%B3%CE%B9%CE%BF%CF%82_1.jpg (I've edited the image slightly to fit the purpose of the article).

I put the link in a collapsible so it wouldn't stretch screens. ~Zyn

Also, brace for a revamp of SCP-4586 file.

Thanks for the support, everyone!

item.title: Junior Staff Post
item.content:encoded:
The image used in this article was non-complaint with the SCP wiki's license (CC-BY-SA 3.0) as the source came from here. Thus, the image has been removed.

EDIT: The image has been replaced with a cc-compliant alternative.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This almost reminds me of 354, what with the tale embedded into the SCP itself. It is a bit long for a SCP, though there certainly much longer articles out there. Interviews are a treat and build up character and provide a solid insight into what Murthwallen was without needing to read the tale. Also, that tale is awesome and is now part of my headcanon for the Factory. A little critique is that the redacted interview feels odd. Are you working on it, or is it just there? If there is not going to be anything there in the future, I think it should just be removed.

I love the picture btw, don't change it. It sets the mood perfectly to be upended.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is actually an interesting skip but I think it needs more background history, the skeleton refusing to talk about his origins makes interviews pretty dull to me altough the fact that he can create anomalous stuff can make this entry much more rich if you exploit it imo. Also, the picture is just too goofy for the article, maybe you should change or remove it.
I liked it tho but I'm not voting for now, will come back later to see if you improve it :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Changed it, thanks!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Pretty iffy to slap something of Level 5 importance to an interview log. At most, I'd leave it at a level 4.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks, I've changed the file accordingly!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thank you for the thorough feedback!

I've shortened the containment procedures into something more breviloquent. I'll work more on the file, and try to rectify the problems you've mentioned.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I recommend that the names of those that are taking in the transcripts being bold than italicized. Also, the last interview collapsible should change to O5 Command instead of High Command, which is what the GOC refers to their higher ups.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is far below the level of quality I look for in mainlist SCPs.

+ show block
– hide block

The SCProcs are far too long, focusing on a plethora of mundane, basic security elements; it neglects the "Special" in "Special Containment Procedures", and is a bore to read because of it. It's primarily composed of the fluff most would leave out, so that readers wouldn't be tempted to simply scroll past them.

The description, likewise, devotes extra lines to further detail its ability to see, while not addressing the basic logic absent in a skeleton being incapacitated by electricity.

The interviews are of chief concern. The character displays no actual personality beyond "talks kind of smart", and with how little actual plot progression there is with the drip-feed of information, I've the impression I did a whole lot of reading with little to show for it. Good dialogue reveals character, moves the story forward, or preferably both; there's nowheres near enough of either to make the interactions enjoyable or memorable.

By the end, it's clear that he's waiting for something - but what that may be, I couldn't begin to guess. Had it been, overall, something I was able to be invested in, perhaps that could have been a solid ending, with the anticipation that something of note is about to happen. Unfortunately, it just feels like there's no payoff to be had.


As-is, in its current state, I am genuinely suprised this survived the voting process, let alone be rated in the double digits. -1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
More please.
I enjoyed reading it alltogether and was slightly annoyed Overwatch removed the final interview.
That said, I got the Ol' Blackwood vibe from him, but that's just the gentlemen side I guess.
Frankly I'd love to see more. Heck, maybe even in the way of Blackwood, tales of his past or so. Or his daily routine in his cell.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks for the feedback!

I've corrected the dialogue issues you mentioned and made the document a little more technical by changing the phrases you brought up.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
When he kept dodging the questions they were throwing at him, I thought it was Dr. Bright with SCP-963 on a skeleton, playing some kind of big trick.

Upvoted, even though it wasn’t that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
With that picture I wanted to like this, but the tone and dialogue failed me too many times for me to like this.

Here are gripes:

+ show block
– hide block
  • The containment procedures are too long for such an easily containable guys, and are a slog to get through.
  • Tone breaks at multiple intervals, saying vague or unprofessional things like “is knowledgeable on several subjects, especially those of a scientific nature,” or “it outright refuses to answer.”
  • You use an IQ test for intelligence, which is a pet peeve of mine. IQ tests really aren’t conducive to intelligence.
  • The skeleton (almost literally) says “good sir” every other sentence and if it was supposed to be humorous I’d like it but this is supposed to be historically accurate? It really put me off
  • Dialogue problems:

can you explain what are you?

perhaps an…unique specimen

I shall satiate you thrist for information.

  • Conceptually it’s a skeleton researcher who makes cool gadgets. Not incredibly interesting.

This article isn’t low-quality, but still fails to work for me.

Edit: Changed to novote. The corrections are good and you’re skilled at slowly developing a story, which is great. But the overall concept just didn’t really seem that interesting to me (he’s an inventor who’s a skeleton).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm impressed that a new author was able to write something of this quality! Good job, I can't wait to see what else you make!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I have a weakness for spookyboi's1 and this just plays on that weakness like a fiddle.

+1, my only regret is that I couldn't +2.

Footnotes
1. skeletons

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Holy h*ck, that picture is god tier. This is a good skip.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Just corrected it, thanks!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I am glad that it came together. You did an amazing job!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Looks pretty good! Minor nitpick, it's "amenstics", not " amnesiacs".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'd like to thank everyone involved in assisting me with this article, especially XaiviarNightwing!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Alright, thanks!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: james-anderson and scp-001 aren’t tags, so I deleted those, I recommend deleting all tags and waiting for the tag team to take care of it instead.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I was worried it would be just another skeletal skip, but WOW this was a fantastic read. My main issue is that it ended. I would die heh for more of this. +1!

Edit: I kinda would prefer it without the picture, as it looks pretty goofy, but I digress.
